title: Logical error: 'Bad cast from type DB::PlainObjectStorage<DB::S3ObjectStorage> to DB::S3ObjectStorage'
description:
### Describe the bug
Easy to reproduce.
### How to reproduce
Start MinIO with `tests/docker_scripts/setup_minio.sh stateless` command. Then set these settings in config.xml:
```xml
<storage_configuration>
<disks>
<disk0>
<type>object_storage</type>
<object_storage_type>s3</object_storage_type>
<metadata_type>plain</metadata_type>
<endpoint>http://localhost:11111/test</endpoint>
<access_key_id>clickhouse</access_key_id>
<secret_access_key>clickhouse</secret_access_key>
</disk0>
</disks>
</storage_configuration>
<allowed_disks_for_table_engines>disk0</allowed_disks_for_table_engines>
```
This is my users.xml: [users.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23262319/users.txt)
Create the table in Spark with: [spark.py](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23262383/spark.py)
Then run the statements in ClickHouse:
```sql
CREATE TABLE t0 ENGINE = IcebergS3(s3, format = 'Parquet', filename = 't0/test/t0');
SELECT 1 FROM icebergS3(s3, format = 'Parquet', filename = 't0/test/t0', structure = 'c0 Int', SETTINGS disk = 'disk0') tx; --Logical error
```
The table does exist in MinIO, so the statement should run successfully?
### Error message and/or stacktrace
Stack trace:
```
<Fatal> : Logical error: 'Bad cast from type DB::PlainObjectStorage<DB::S3ObjectStorage> to DB::S3ObjectStorage'.
<Fatal> : Stack trace (when copying this message, always include the lines below):

2. src/Common/Exception.h:123: DB::Exception::Exception(String&&, int, String, bool) @ 0x000000000d60df4e
3. src/Common/Exception.h:58: DB::Exception::Exception(PreformattedMessage&&, int) @ 0x000000000d60d951
4. src/Common/Exception.h:141: DB::Exception::Exception<String, String>(int, FormatStringHelperImpl<std::type_identity<String>::type, std::type_identity<String>::type>, String&&, String&&) @ 0x000000000d60bcb6
5. src/Common/assert_cast.h:48: DB::S3ObjectStorage const& assert_cast<DB::S3ObjectStorage const&, DB::IObjectStorage&>(DB::IObjectStorage&) @ 0x000000001938198e
6. src/Storages/ObjectStorage/S3/Configuration.cpp:309: DB::StorageS3Configuration::fromDisk(String const&, absl::lts_20250512::InlinedVector<std::shared_ptr<DB::IAST>, 7ul, std::allocator<std::shared_ptr<DB::IAST>>>&, std::shared_ptr<DB::Context const>, bool) @ 0x0000000019379a31
7. src/Storages/ObjectStorage/DataLakes/DataLakeConfiguration.h:356: DB::DataLakeConfiguration<DB::StorageS3Configuration, DB::IcebergMetadata>::fromDisk(String const&, absl::lts_20250512::InlinedVector<std::shared_ptr<DB::IAST>, 7ul, std::allocator<std::shared_ptr<DB::IAST>>>&, std::shared_ptr<DB::Context const>, bool) @ 0x0000000017350f8f
8. src/Storages/ObjectStorage/StorageObjectStorageConfiguration.cpp:84: DB::StorageObjectStorageConfiguration::initialize(DB::StorageObjectStorageConfiguration&, absl::lts_20250512::InlinedVector<std::shared_ptr<DB::IAST>, 7ul, std::allocator<std::shared_ptr<DB::IAST>>>&, std::shared_ptr<DB::Context const>, bool) @ 0x00000000191dd3a2
9. src/TableFunctions/TableFunctionObjectStorage.h:58: DB::TableFunctionObjectStorage<DB::IcebergS3Definition, DB::DataLakeConfiguration<DB::StorageS3Configuration, DB::IcebergMetadata>, true>::parseArgumentsImpl(absl::lts_20250512::InlinedVector<std::shared_ptr<DB::IAST>, 7ul, std::allocator<std::shared_ptr<DB::IAST>>>&, std::shared_ptr<DB::Context const> const&) @ 0x000000001737f10f

11. src/Analyzer/Resolve/QueryAnalyzer.cpp:3901: DB::QueryAnalyzer::resolveTableFunction(std::shared_ptr<DB::IQueryTreeNode>&, DB::IdentifierResolveScope&, DB::QueryExpressionsAliasVisitor&, bool) @ 0x000000001a2625f8
12. src/Analyzer/Resolve/QueryAnalyzer.cpp:4510: DB::QueryAnalyzer::resolveQueryJoinTreeNode(std::shared_ptr<DB::IQueryTreeNode>&, DB::IdentifierResolveScope&, DB::QueryExpressionsAliasVisitor&) @ 0x000000001a2804cd

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER, name TEXT, created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, data TEXT);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, data) VALUES (1, 'test1', NULL);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, data) VALUES (2, 'test2', 'sample data');
SELECT id, name, created_at, data FROM test_table WHERE data IS NULL;
SELECT id, name FROM test_table WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE name LIKE 'test%') ORDER BY id DESC;
key_bug_features: Bad cast from PlainObjectStorage to S3ObjectStorage
